commit.message:
chore: update GitHub Actions for Node.js 24 compatibility

- Add FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24 environment variable
- Update Node.js version in build job from 18 to 22
- Prepare for Node.js 20 deprecation (June 2nd, 2026)
- Actions already support Node.js 24:
  * actions/checkout@v4
  * actions/upload-artifact@v4
  * actions/download-artifact@v4
  * google-github-actions/auth@v2
  * google-github-actions/setup-gcloud@v2
  * hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add TIMER_RADIUS = 34 and SCORE_RING_RADIUS = 68 constants
- Derive CIRCUMFERENCE from TIMER_RADIUS
- Derive scoreCircumference from SCORE_RING_RADIUS

  2 * π * r formulas for timer and score ring circles

commit.message:
feat: implement OpenViking-inspired capabilities (hotness score, used() feedback, L0/L1/L2, fast/quality paths, memory taxonomy, tool stats)

Borrowed 5 high-value capabilities from volcengine/OpenViking, maximally
leveraging openJiuwen framework built-ins (AgenticRetriever for quality path,
rrf_fusion framework already available).

New capabilities:
- Hotness Score: sigmoid(log1p(active_count)) × exp(-λ×age_days) blended
  into RRF fusion at alpha=0.2 (context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py)
- used() feedback API: POST /context/used increments active_count on
  confirmed context items, driving Hotness Score
- MemoryCategory 6-taxonomy: profile/preferences/entities/events/cases/patterns
  with AggregationRequest.category_filter support
- ContextLevel L0/L1/L2: ABSTRACT/OVERVIEW/DETAIL levels with
  AggregationRequest.max_level progressive expansion
- fast/quality dual-path: mode='quality' activates AgenticRetriever
  (openJiuwen-native) for complex tasks; mode='fast' (default) uses
  HybridRetriever for low-latency simple queries
- Tool Performance Memory: ToolContextGovernor.record_tool_result() +
  POST /tools/result endpoint; unreliable tools rank lower in select_tools()

Files changed:
- context_agent/models/context.py: +ContextLevel, +MemoryCategory enums,
  +ContextItem.{level,category,active_count,updated_at} fields
- context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py: new hotness pure function module
- context_agent/core/retrieval/search_coordinator.py: hotness-blended RRF
- context_agent/core/memory/working_memory.py: +mark_used() method
- context_agent/core/memory/tiered_router.py: +record_usage() method
- context_agent/core/retrieval/tool_governor.py: +record_tool_result(), +_sort_by_success_rate()
- context_agent/adapters/ltm_adapter.py: +agentic_search() with AgenticRetriever fallback
- context_agent/orchestration/context_aggregator.py: +mode/max_level/category_filter params
- context_agent/api/schemas.py: +ContextUsedRequest/Response, +ToolResultRequest/Response
- context_agent/api/http_handler.py: +POST /context/used, +POST /tools/result
- context_agent/api/router.py: +mark_used(), +record_tool_result() facade methods
- tests/unit/test_openviking_capabilities.py: 38 new unit tests (all passing)
- docs/architecture-design.md: +Appendix D with capability details
- docs/agent-integration-guide.md: +Section 9 with usage guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use dynamic year in footer copyright

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: safe SMTP port parsing and upgrade MimeKit to 4.15.1 to fix CRLF injection vulnerability

- Use int.TryParse instead of int.Parse for SMTP port configuration to avoid FormatException
- Upgrade MimeKit from 4.8.0 to 4.15.1 to fix GHSA-g7hc-96xr-gvvx (CRLF injection in quoted local-part)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement complete user registration web application

- Clean Architecture with Domain, Application, Infrastructure, API, Web projects
- User entity with extensive personal and academic information fields
- JWT authentication with ASP.NET Core Identity
- Email service (console-based when SMTP not configured)
- EF Core with SQLite database
- ASP.NET Core MVC frontend with Bootstrap 5 UI
- Registration form with personal + academic information
- User profile page with edit functionality
- Session-based authentication in the web layer

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  naming conventions

  input) for structural compliance

  per APS DSL specification

commit.message:
polish: add depth and shadow to CardTopic for a less flat UI (#262)

* Initial plan

* polish: add depth and shadow to CardTopic for a less flat UI

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: move shadow props to outer wrapper so they aren't clipped by overflow:hidden

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify card View and add white background

Collapse the multi-line <View> style into a single-line array for readability and add backgroundColor: '#fff' to the card style. The white background fixes rendering (e.g. shadow/overlay) and ensures consistent card appearance across platforms; no other behavior changes were made.

* Wrap Animated.View to preserve shadow

Move the shadow/container styles to a non-animated outer View and confine the scale/opacity animation to an inner Animated.View (new animatedWrapper style). Removed the shadow backgroundColor so the inner gradient is visible. This prevents the shadow/elevation from scaling with the card during the bounce animation and fixes related visual artifacts/clipping on Android.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Open-Meteo SEAS5 seasonal forecast integration (weather outlook) (#252)

* Initial plan

* feat: Open-Meteo SEAS5 seasonal forecast integration

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use ensemble-mean fields & update parsing

Switch the seasonal outlook flow to use ensemble-mean variables returned by the API instead of aggregating per-member values. Changes include:

- services: replace member-aggregation with direct reading of *_mean variables; add monthlyValue helper; remove mean/ensemble member logic; change cache table to seasonal_outlook_cache_v2; use UTC for toYearMonth; drop models param from request.
- types: update MonthlyOutlookEntry to tempMeanC and windSpeedMeanKmh and adjust variable names used throughout.
- UI: SeasonalOutlookScreen now shows average labels, uses tempMeanC/windSpeedMeanKmh, replaces the disclaimer banner with SectionSubHeader, and tweaks flag/wind labels/layout.
- store: WeatherOutlookStore minor refactors (import ordering, wording), use tempMeanC when computing summaries.
- tests: update expectations and fixtures to match *_mean variables and updated field names; adjust formatting and minor test helpers.
- misc: add workspace settings entries (Meteo, precip) and small formatting tweaks.

Note: cache table name changed (seasonal_outlook_cache_v2) — existing cached data may need migration or clearing.

* fix: address PR review feedback on weather outlook feature

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use monthly mean temps and wind; update UI/tests

Switch seasonal outlook from ensemble daily max/min to ensemble monthly mean values and propagate that change through parsing, store logic, UI and tests.

- services: Replace temperature_2m_max/min and wind_speed_10m_max with temperature_2m_mean and wind_speed_10m_mean in MONTHLY_VARIABLES and parseMonthlyResponse; update MonthlyOutlookEntry shape (tempMeanC, windSpeedMeanKmh).
- store: WeatherOutlookStore now uses tempMeanC instead of averaging tempMaxC/tempMinC when generating human-readable summaries.
- UI: SeasonalOutlookScreen displays the mean temperature (adds "Avg" label), uses tempMeanC and windSpeedMeanKmh for displays/flags, and adjusts icon colors to SECONDARY_ACCENT.
- SettingsModal: Extract makeStyles to generate themed styles, memoize them with useMemo, replace many inline color objects with themed style references, add optionsContainerRow and fileItemSelected helpers, and tidy up button/label styling.
- Footer: SolarCycleNotification effect dependency updated to include the weatherOutlook store reference.
- Tests: Updated WeatherOutlookStore tests and sample data to reflect tempMeanC and windSpeedMeanKmh field names and updated parsing expectations.

These changes align the app with the API's monthly-mean variables and consolidate theming/styling for Settings modal.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
TTS cleaning v2: filler normalization, Bowzer/Gumba phonetics, short phrase padding, nocache bypass, period-comma conversion. Ralph loop R10-R24 avg 67pct acceptable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add missing workflow files from OSPO actions

Added standard workflows used across OSPO GitHub Actions:

- auto-labeler.yml: Automatically labels PRs based on release-drafter config
- copilot-setup-steps.yml: Documents Copilot CLI setup for contributors
- linter.yaml: Runs super-linter on PRs to enforce code quality
- pr-title.yml: Validates PR titles follow conventional commit format
- scorecard.yml: Runs OpenSSF Scorecard security analysis
- stale.yaml: Closes stale issues after 35 days of inactivity
- release-drafter.yml: Configuration for auto-labeler and release notes

These workflows provide:
- Automated PR labeling for release notes
- Code quality enforcement via linting
- Security scanning via Scorecard
- Stale issue management
- PR title validation

Brings pr-conflict-detector in line with other OSPO actions like
issue-metrics, stale-repos, contributors, etc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
